rent.note: furnished 1BR in nomad area (Namba, Shinsaibashi, Tennoji); central 1BR ~JPY 80,000-120,000 ($560-840); significantly cheaper than Tokyo

utilities.note: electric + water + internet; Japan internet excellent ~JPY 5,000/mo; seasonal AC/heating costs

groceries.note: home cooking; Osaka is Japan's kitchen — very affordable local food; JPY 25,000-35,000/mo

diningOut.note: Osaka street food paradise — takoyaki/okonomiyaki ~JPY 600-800; mid restaurant JPY 1,500-3,000; cheaper than Tokyo

transit.note: Osaka metro monthly ~JPY 6,600-8,000 (~$45-55); compact city, walkable areas

coworking.note: hot-desk monthly; smaller coworking market than Tokyo; expect JPY 20,000-35,000/mo

simData.note: SIM-only plan (IIJmio/Mineo/OCN); unlimited data ~JPY 2,500-3,500/mo; same as Tokyo

misc.note: gym (~JPY 5,000), toiletries, entertainment, street food, cultural outings, buffer

accomSeasonality._doc: 12 monthly multipliers for RENT ONLY (Jan..Dec). 1.0 = base. Osaka furnished lets are stable year-round for monthly rentals.

evidence.quote: monthly living costs in Osaka come to around $1,352 including rent, or roughly $751 excluding it
Expect to pay about $733 for a central one-bedroom, or $467 outside the center; rent can range from ¥40,000 to ¥120,000 (approximately $350-$1,100 USD) per month
Expect to spend about $300 monthly on groceries; single person spends $200–$400/month on groceries

evidence.claim: Osaka street food costs and dining
evidence.quote: Osaka's street food (takoyaki, okonomiyaki) costs $6–$8; dining out at a mid-range restaurant costs roughly $35.00 for two
